'''Tom Johnstone''' (born 13 August 1995) is an [[England national rugby league team|England]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2015 RFL Championship|Championship]]. Johnstone was the winner of the [[Man of Steel Awards|Super League Young Player of the Year]] in 2016.

==Background==
Johnstone was born in Germany and is of Scottish descent.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.totalrl.com/getting-know-tom-johnstone/|title=Getting to know: Tom Johnstone |date=2017-01-24|work=Total Rugby League|accessdate=2018-06-03|language=en-US}}</ref>

With his father in the Army, Johnstone was born in Germany. Johnstone has lived in England since he was very young; as a junior, he played for Stanningley Amateur Rugby League Club. He attended [[Pudsey Grangefield School]] and represented the City of Leeds sprinting team at Indoor athletics.

== Playing career==
===Wakefield Trinity===
Johnstone plays at Wing or Centre. He is noted as one of the fastest players in the game. He has also represented England at under 19's level. He made his professional début for Wakefield Trinity against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 2014 during a Boxing day friendly at Headingley.  He made his full Super League début for an injury-hit Wakefield Trinity side in their 2015 Round 7 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. He played 13 times in his first season at Wakefield Trinity, scoring 9 tries. He scored his first Wakefield Trinity try against St Helens in a 44-4 defeat. In his second season 2016 he scored one length of the field effort that was considered to be the try of the season.

The Wakefield Trinity club signed him to a four-season deal, longer than the usual length for the club.  A young talent in English Rugby League, Johnstone is regarded as a fast player in the [[Super League]] and also a finisher.  Johnstone received his first International call up against France in 2018, he scored a hat-trick of tries on his debut.  Johnstone was a favorite 2019 to become Super League's top try-scorer until he was injured.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity/wakefield-trinity-tom-johnstone-has-half-an-eye-on-top-try-accolade-1-9561844|title=Wakefield Trinity: Tom Johnstone has 'half an eye' on top-try accolade|work=Yorkshire Evening Post|language=en|accessdate=2019-02-27}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2021 Super League season, Johnstone scored two tries and received the man of the match award in Wakefield's 28–22 loss against Leeds.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/mar/27/wakefield-leeds-super-league-match-report/|title=Leeds roar back to deny Tom Johnstone-inspired Wakefield in Super League|website=The Guardian}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 5 July 2022, Johnstone signed a two-year deal to join the [[Catalans Dragons]] starting on 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/wakefield-trinitys-tom-johnstones-future-confirmed-55618/|title=Wakefield Trinity's Tom Johnstone's future confirmed|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
On 15 July 2022, Johnstone was ruled out for the remainder of the 2022 Super League season with a groin injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62185809#:~:text=Wakefield%20Trinity%20winger%20Tom%20Johnstone,Catalans%20Dragons%20earlier%20this%20month./|title=Tom Johnstone: Wakefield Trinity winger ruled out for season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2023 Super League season, Johnstone made his club debut for Catalans against his former club Wakefield Trinity. Johnstone would score a [[hat-trick]] in Catalans 38-24 victory. In round 5 of the competition, he scored another hat-trick in Catalans 26-12 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64953477|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 26-12 Hull KR - hosts continue perfect start to season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> On 1 April 2023, he scored his 100th career try in a 22–18 win against [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 22-18 Castleford Tigers -Mike McMeeken scores late winner |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65113050 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=8 April 2023 |date=1 April 2023}}</ref>
In round 13 of the 2023 Super League season, Johnstone scored two tries against his former club Wakefield Trinity as Catalans won 36-6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65697883|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 36-6 Wakefield Trinity - Tom Johnstone punishes former club|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
The following week, Johnstone scored a [[hat-trick]] in Catalans 46-22 victory over Wigan at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65753905|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 22-46 Catalans - Johnstone hat-trick helps Dragons move top|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Johnstone played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity (re-join)===
On 17 May 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a four-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Wakefield Trinity|date=17 May 2024|accessdate=29 Sep 2024|title=Tom Johnstone Returns!!|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/tom-johnstone-returns/}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|work=telegraph.co.uk|date=17 October 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

== Injuries==
Johnstone has suffered two career-threatening [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injuries, one in each leg. The first was in 2017, the second was in 2019 from which he recovered in time to take part in 2020's [[Super League XXV]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
